gwt-0001.name: the_worker_begins_job_execution
gwt-0001.given: A hosted worker job has been dequeued and the job's input artifacts (schemas, dag.json, crawl.db, specs, bridge artifacts) reside in object storage under the project's storage prefix
gwt-0001.then: the worker downloads all declared input artifacts from object storage to an ephemeral local workspace at /workspace/.cw9/ before invoking any core pipeline function

gwt-0002.name: the_core_function_returns_(whether_passi
gwt-0002.given: A hosted worker job has completed core pipeline function execution and output artifacts exist in the ephemeral /workspace/.cw9/ workspace
gwt-0002.when: the core function returns (whether passing or failing)
gwt-0002.then: the worker uploads all output artifacts (specs/*.tla, specs/*.cfg, specs/*_traces.json, bridge/*_bridge_artifacts.json, sessions/*, tests/generated/*) to object storage under the project's storage prefix before the container exits

gwt-0003.name: the_worker_reconstructs_its_execution_co
gwt-0003.given: A hosted worker container starts fresh for a new job with no pre-existing state, and all required artifacts are available in object storage and Postgres
gwt-0003.when: the worker reconstructs its execution context
gwt-0003.then: the worker fully reconstructs all required state (ProjectContext, RegistryDag, CrawlStore connection) from object storage downloads and Postgres reads without requiring any session affinity, warm-up cache, or connection to a previous worker process

gwt-0004.name: projectcontext.hosted(project_id,_worksp
gwt-0004.given: A hosted worker has been given a project_id, an ephemeral workspace path (/workspace), and an object storage client handle
gwt-0004.then: a frozen ProjectContext is returned with state_root set to /workspace/.cw9/, template_dir and tools_dir pointing to container-baked paths, and a storage_client attribute available for downstream download/upload operations; the three existing factory modes (self_hosting, external, installed) are unchanged

gwt-0005.name: run_loop()_begins_execution_for_the_gwt
gwt-0005.given: A hosted heavy-tla worker has been assigned a loop job for a single GWT ID and has downloaded dag.json, context files, and the PlusCal template from object storage
gwt-0005.when: run_loop() begins execution for the GWT
gwt-0005.then: _make_client() is called exactly once at job start, producing a single ClaudeSDKClient that is reused across all retry attempts (up to max_retries=8) for that GWT, maintaining full multi-turn conversation context across attempts

gwt-0006.name: the_retry_loop_exits_(whether_by_pass,_f
gwt-0006.given: A loop job has an active ClaudeSDKClient created at job start via _make_client()
gwt-0006.when: the retry loop exits (whether by PASS, FAIL, or exhausting max_retries)
gwt-0006.then: safe_disconnect() is called in a finally block ensuring the SDK client is disconnected regardless of outcome, and no SDK session state persists after the worker container exits

gwt-0007.name: each_worker_processes_its_assigned_gwt
gwt-0007.given: Two or more loop jobs are executing concurrently for different GWT IDs within the same project
gwt-0007.when: each worker processes its assigned GWT
gwt-0007.then: each loop worker creates its own independent ClaudeSDKClient and reads its own copy of dag.json from object storage; no SDK client, in-memory DAG, or CrawlStore connection is shared between workers processing different GWTs

gwt-0008.name: the_worker_initializes_its_llm_client_vi
gwt-0008.given: A hosted worker container is starting a loop or crawl job that requires the Claude Agent SDK
gwt-0008.when: the worker initializes its LLM client via _make_client() or _build_llm_fn()
gwt-0008.then: the CLAUDECODE environment variable is consumed from the container's injected environment (not inherited from a parent CLI process), and the SDK client is initialized with credentials sourced exclusively from that environment variable

gwt-0009.name: compile_compose_verify()_creates_/tmp/cw
gwt-0009.given: A heavy-tla loop worker is executing compile_compose_verify() for a GWT attempt inside a container
gwt-0009.when: compile_compose_verify() creates /tmp/cw9_XXXX/ and invokes java pcal.trans and java tlc2.TLC
gwt-0009.then: the temp directory is isolated to that container's /tmp with no cross-job contamination; upon PASS the verified .tla and .cfg files are read from the temp dir and uploaded to object storage as part of the worker's output-upload step before the container exits

gwt-0010.name: the_job_lifecycle_progresses_through_the
gwt-0010.given: A job record exists in Postgres with status PENDING
gwt-0010.when: the job lifecycle progresses through the hosted execution pipeline
gwt-0010.then: the job transitions through exactly the states PENDING → QUEUED (on enqueue) → RUNNING (on worker dequeue) → one of: PASSED (on LoopResult.PASS or successful completion), FAILED (on LoopResult.FAIL or unhandled exception after max_retries), or RETRYING (on LoopResult.RETRY, transitioning back to RUNNING for the next attempt); each transition is written atomically to Postgres

gwt-0011.name: run_loop()_receives_the_pass_result
gwt-0011.given: A loop job attempt has produced LoopResult.PASS from OneShotLoop.process_response() and the verified .tla file is available in the worker's workspace
gwt-0011.then: run_tlc_simulate() is called with the verified spec (up to 300s timeout), the resulting simulation traces are written to specs/{gwt_id}_sim_traces.json in the workspace, and that file is included in the output-upload batch before the container exits

gwt-0012.name: schemaextractor.extract()_runs_inside_cm
gwt-0012.given: An extract job has been dequeued and the project's schema/*.json files and existing dag.json have been downloaded from object storage to the worker's workspace
gwt-0012.then: the DAG is rebuilt from schema files using pure Python with no LLM call and no subprocess, registered nodes from the previous DAG are merged via merge_registered_nodes(), and the updated dag.json is uploaded to object storage; total execution completes in under 10 seconds

gwt-0013.name: run_bridge()_executes
gwt-0013.given: A bridge job has been dequeued and the worker has downloaded specs/{gwt_id}.tla, specs/{gwt_id}_traces.json, and specs/{gwt_id}_sim_traces.json from object storage to the workspace
gwt-0013.then: the TLA+ spec is parsed using pure Python regex via parse_spec(); translate_state_vars(), translate_actions(), translate_invariants_to_verifiers(), translate_invariants_to_assertions(), and translate_traces() populate a BridgeResult; bridge/{gwt_id}_bridge_artifacts.json is written to the workspace and uploaded to object storage; no LLM or subprocess is invoked

gwt-0014.name: the_scheduler_selects_a_worker_node_for_
gwt-0014.given: A loop job is assigned to a worker node in the execution plane
gwt-0014.when: the scheduler selects a worker node for the job
gwt-0014.then: the selected node must be classified as heavy-tla, meaning it has a Java JRE installed, tla2tools.jar available at the container-baked tools_dir, and the Claude Agent SDK accessible via environment credentials; the job timeout must accommodate up to 8 retries × 300s TLC check + 60s pcal.trans per attempt (approximately 40 minutes total)

gwt-0015.name: run_test_gen_loop()_executes
gwt-0015.given: A gen-tests job has been dequeued with a target language profile (Python/TypeScript/Rust/Go), and the worker has downloaded bridge_artifacts.json, dag.json, and relevant spec files from object storage
gwt-0015.then: the worker invokes the Claude Agent SDK for plan/review/codegen LLM passes via call_llm(), then calls the language-specific verify_test_file() which runs the appropriate subprocess (pytest / npx jest / cargo test / go test); on success, the generated test file is uploaded to object storage under tests/generated/

gwt-0016.name: run_test_gen_loop()_begins_the_llm_gener
gwt-0016.given: A gen-tests worker has initialized a language profile and downloaded all required artifacts
gwt-0016.when: run_test_gen_loop() begins the LLM generation sequence
gwt-0016.then: exactly three sequential LLM calls are made in order—Pass 1 (build_test_plan_prompt → call_llm), Pass 2 (build_review_prompt → call_llm), Pass 3 (build_codegen_prompt → call_llm)—followed by extract_code_from_response() and verify_test_file(); if verification fails, a retry loop begins using build_retry_prompt until the test file passes all checks or max retries are exhausted

gwt-0017.name: crawlorchestrator.run()_executes_the_dfs
gwt-0017.given: A crawl job has been dequeued and crawl.db (containing skeleton FnRecord entries with SKELETON_ONLY DO fields) has been downloaded from object storage to the worker's workspace
gwt-0017.when: CrawlOrchestrator.run() executes the DFS extraction loop
gwt-0017.then: for each skeleton record, exactly one Claude Agent SDK call (claude-sonnet-4-6) is made via extract_one() to fill IN:DO:OUT behavioral data; on full completion, the populated crawl.db is uploaded to object storage and the Postgres job record is updated to PASSED

gwt-0018.name: cmd_ingest()_runs_the_language-specific_
gwt-0018.given: An ingest job has been dequeued with access to the project's source code tree and an empty or existing crawl.db
gwt-0018.then: the scanner produces skeleton FnRecord entries written to crawl.db and RESOURCE nodes written to dag.json using pure filesystem reads and DB writes with no LLM calls or subprocess invocations; the updated crawl.db and dag.json are uploaded to object storage; total execution completes in under 10 seconds

gwt-0019.name: the_api_handler_processes_the_request
gwt-0019.given: A user submits POST /projects to the control plane API with project configuration
gwt-0019.when: the API handler processes the request
gwt-0019.then: a Postgres project record is created with a unique project_id, an object storage prefix is provisioned for the project, and the response returns the project_id and storage prefix; no .cw9/ filesystem directory is created on any worker node—cmd_init's local filesystem operations are replaced entirely by this API-side operation

gwt-0020.name: registrydag.load()_or_registrydag.save()
gwt-0020.given: A hosted worker needs to read or write the project's RegistryDag
gwt-0020.when: RegistryDag.load() or RegistryDag.save() is called in hosted context
gwt-0020.then: the DAG is read from / written to a Postgres JSON column (or normalized tables) rather than a local dag.json file; the in-memory RegistryDag object and all its methods (add_node, add_edge, query_relevant, register_gwt, register_requirement, merge_registered_nodes, etc.) are unchanged—only the persistence backend differs

gwt-0021.name: crawlstore_is_instantiated_in_hosted_mod
gwt-0021.given: A hosted worker needs to read or write crawl.db behavioral records
gwt-0021.when: CrawlStore is instantiated in hosted mode
gwt-0021.then: the CrawlStore adapter connects to Postgres instead of SQLite, maintains the same context-manager scoping (connection opened on __enter__, closed on __exit__), equivalent WAL-mode isolation, and exposes the identical public interface (get_record, upsert_record, insert_record, get_all_uuids, get_pending_uuids, get_stale_records, get_card_text, etc.) so that no callers require modification

gwt-0022.name: the_control_plane_builds_the_execution_p
gwt-0022.given: A user submits POST /projects/{id}/runs to the control plane API to trigger a full greenfield pipeline run
gwt-0022.when: the control plane builds the execution plan
gwt-0022.then: a job DAG is persisted in Postgres with stages ordered as: extract → register → loop (one job per GWT, all loop jobs parallelizable) → bridge (one job per verified GWT, depends on its corresponding loop job) → gen-tests (one job per verified GWT, depends on its corresponding bridge job); _resolve_gwt_ids logic determines the set of loop jobs; cmd_pipeline's sequential orchestration is replaced entirely by this dependency-driven job scheduler

gwt-0023.name: _register_payload()_executes
gwt-0023.given: A register job has been dequeued with the project's dag.json and criterion_bindings.json downloaded from object storage, and a JSON payload describing new GWTs or requirements
gwt-0023.then: the RegistryDag is mutated in memory (GWT/REQ nodes added, edges wired, closure recomputed via register_gwt or register_requirement), criterion_bindings.json is updated for idempotency via save_bindings(), and both dag.json and criterion_bindings.json are uploaded back to object storage; no LLM call is made

gwt-0024.name: write_result_file()_is_called_with_the_j
gwt-0024.given: A loop or gen-tests worker job has completed (PASS, FAIL, or max-retries exhausted) and a result is available
gwt-0024.when: write_result_file() is called with the job outcome
gwt-0024.then: in the hosted system, the structured result (gwt_id, result, attempts, error) is written to a Postgres job_results table and the raw LLM session logs ({gwt_id}_attempt{N}.txt, {gwt_id}_result.json) are uploaded to object storage for debugging; the local .cw9/sessions/ write is replaced entirely by these two operations

gwt-0025.name: run_gwt_author()_executes
gwt-0025.given: A gwt-author job has been dequeued with research notes, a populated crawl.db, and dag.json downloaded from object storage
gwt-0025.then: exactly one LLM call is made via _call_llm(), the response is parsed by parse_gwt_response(), depends_on references are validated against crawl.db UUIDs via validate_depends_on(), and the resulting GWT JSON is returned to the control plane API caller; the job completes in under 60 seconds

gwt-0026.name: query_context()_is_called_for_the_gwt_id
gwt-0026.given: A loop worker has downloaded dag.json and optionally crawl.db to its workspace and is building the LLM prompt for a GWT attempt
gwt-0026.when: query_context() is called for the GWT ID
gwt-0026.then: relevant DAG nodes are retrieved via dag.query_relevant(gwt_id), any matching FnRecord cards are fetched from crawl.db via CrawlStore.get_record(), the combined context is formatted by format_prompt_context(), and _build_prompt() incorporates the result; if crawl.db is absent from object storage, the prompt is built with DAG context only without error

gwt-0027.name: build_retry_prompt()_constructs_the_next
gwt-0027.given: A loop job attempt has returned LoopResult.RETRY because TLC found a counterexample, parse_counterexample() has produced a structured trace, and classify_tlc_error() has categorised the failure
gwt-0027.when: build_retry_prompt() constructs the next attempt's prompt
gwt-0027.then: the retry prompt includes the counterexample trace translated by translate_counterexample() (human-readable state sequence), the TLC error classification, and the previous attempt's PlusCal text; this enriched prompt is sent to the persistent ClaudeSDKClient so the LLM observes the full correction history across all attempts for the GWT
